title: Fix 'Payment service not ready' error on payment form submission

requirements.summary: Fix the 'Payment service not ready' error by ensuring the actor is fully initialized before allowing payment submission, adding retry logic with exponential back-off, and surfacing a user-friendly loading state when the actor is not yet ready
acceptanceCriteria: Submitting the payment form no longer shows 'Payment service not ready. Please wait a moment and try again.' when the actor is initializing.
The submit button is disabled (with a loading indicator) while the actor is not yet ready, preventing premature submission.
The `useSubmitPayment` mutation in `useQueries.ts` is gated with `enabled`/`mutationFn` guard so it does not execute until the actor instance is non-null.
Transient actor or network errors during submission are retried at least 2 times with exponential back-off before surfacing an error to the user.
After the actor is ready, a valid form submission completes successfully and shows the confirmation screen.

file_operations.description: Modify the useSubmitPayment mutation to gate execution on actor readiness by checking `!!actor` before allowing the mutationFn to fire. Add retry logic with at least 2 retries and exponential back-off (e.g., using React Query's `retry` and `retryDelay` options) to handle transient actor initialization or network errors gracefully. Ensure the mutation does not execute if the actor is null, preventing premature 'Payment service not ready' errors.

Update the PaymentForm component to detect when the actor is not yet ready (using the `isFetching` state from useActor) and disable the submit button with a loading spinner or indicator during actor initialization. Ensure the form does not allow submission until the actor is fully initialized and non-null. Add visual feedback (e.g., 'Connecting to payment service...') so users understand the system is initializing. Wire the mutation's retry state to show appropriate feedback if retries are in progress.
